title: [NSW/QLD/SA] Solar Sharer Offer – 3 Hours of Free Electricity Daily (Up to 24kWh) from 1 July 2026
body:
Deal: [NSW/QLD/SA] Solar Sharer Offer – 3 Hours of Free Electricity Daily (Up to 24kWh) from 1 July 2026

[3dprintdeals] make sure to compare the daily supply rates on these plans..
[SydBoy] That is correct. One of the retailer jacked up supply rate to $1.6 per day which means whether you use the electricity or not the minimum for 3 months will be $145 !
Also, the usage rate out of the hours might be high as well and basically it will kill of any savings you could do with 3 hr free plan !
[Lolitsjoel] 24kwh at say 35c is $8.40. So just smash it all during than
[j1nx] Yep I've seen as high as 172c per day. Wild.
[Rhymenoceros] @31mop: Ahh for me GB- it's going up to 2.37! Jack is in the house
[ganjadeals] Don't get why Victorians can't get it at the same time. Electricity usage is the highest in winter for most people since the state government has moved many households to electricity only. We only get it from October when it's less cold and less heating is required thus less electricity usage.
[Chmmr] Could the negative voter explain their negative? Do Victorians have something else that we don't know about?
[Wolfhound] I am not the negative voter but there are definitely already some victorian plans with 3 hours or 4 hours of free electricity. Most popular one is/was globird but I have not researched for homes without a solar battery.
[Chmmr] Thanks Wolfhound. I don't use solar (I don't own this house), so besides what ganjadeals described in their post, I don't know what we have and why users would have negged them.
[TimApple] The savings is offset by other costs including jacking up peak rate and daily charges.
[Lolitsjoel] Probably because your solar output is quite low during winter and isn’t in the high end positive on the grid to warrant the early winter adaption
[JinXR] The Victorians are paying the CFMEU offshoot billions of dollars instead.
[benjaminv] OVO Energy on my address offers A Free 3 plan allowing free electricity usage from 11 to 2pm. I’ve been on it for nearly two years. Daily charge is 94.7c and rate is 24.3c per kWh. They are doing referrals campaign too.
[RQNinja] If every retailer is providing this, they will have the same benefits as ovo. What can I do do to their 3 plan to make it better?
[Lucian] This scheme is mandated by the AER which is for QLD, NSW, SA, TAS. The Victorian regulator is the ESC, they are bringing in a similar scheme on 1st October.
https://www.energy.vic.gov.au/households/save-energy-and-mon…
[Windows7forever] They will just jack up the daily supply charge to compensate.
[Rogue01] Yeah they already are. Mine is going up 16c a day, and usage too by 9c kWh. One I was looking at increased supply by 60c/day as of July 1st. It's theft, since wholesale rates are usually negative at those hours anyway.
[Graffin] Being on Amber, i've only seen negative rates throughout the Summer months (which seems kinda dumb and wasteful that we get paid to essentially waste power).
During the Winter months, it can be anything from 10 to 40 cents during those times depending on how much renewable energy is in the grid for that day. I'm a lil worried that this is yet another bad Labor policy that will drive up the prices for those on VPP's or using a provider like Amber or Globird. If energy is taken out of the grid for free, surely it means more expensive power for others, or an increase of fees somewhere to make up the difference. I guess only time will tell.
[Rogue01] Yep you are right. Nothing is free. Electricity is a business not a right so we will all pay somehow. How's amber working for you, and what's your setup? I'm about to decide on a battery setup and then retailer…
[Graffin] @Rogue01: Mixed feelings. I joined Amber last September where they were flooded by customers joining because of the home battery scheme, so no one would take calls. Their customer service and support was (and still is) awful.
Because of only being able to communicate via email and live chat it took almost two months to get my Tesla battery onboarded.
Currently have 6.2kWh in panels, a 5kWh inverter and a Powerwall 2 (having a Powerwall 3 installed at the end of this month).
Over the Summer months we banked up >$300 in credit due to a spike on one day giving us almost $200, but during the Winter months we’re eating into that credit by about $30-40 a month.
My only advice is, don’t always trust their SmartShift to make the best decisions for you. A lot of the time it’ll sell power at lower rates despite clearly predicting that export rates will be significantly higher later in the day/night. It may also buy power at ridiculous rates and sell it at a loss. Some times it gets it right, other times it doesn’t, so if you want to maximise your savings/profit, you have to keep checking the app (and hope there isn’t some sort of outage - it’s like every week or so there’s an issue with the app). It can be frustrating, and the $25 per month fee on top of the daily supply charge blows, but for now, it’s still the best deal we can find. Will see how this Summer goes and if we chew through the credit then I’ll explore alternatives like Globird.
It was so much simpler when we were with Origin and got paid a 20c FIT, but it’s now at 4c (capped for the 1st 14kWh, then 2c per kWh) which is pathetic.
[31mop] @Graffin: Although the rates are CRAP, I'm doing really well with GloBird. I've been on there about 40 days now, and I'm averaged over $1 back in my pocket each day. It works if you follow the rules, but you'll get stung if you dont
I have 42kw battery, 15kw inverter, 8kw solar.
[Rogue01] @Graffin: Thanks for the in depth response, really appreciate it. I'm looking at a much bigger battery system to really make use of the peaks and avoid the troughs. I've already got 13kw panels but still getting $180 bills a month in QLD.
I've heard smart shift is unreliable and home assistant seems to be the go to for maximising. Whether I can actually get it all working is another matter though…
I was also considering Globird as an easier less hands on retailer and it looks like Amber pushed a lot over to them too. I think some of the VPPs might be ok too but I just don't know yet.
[tman2020] Are youn saying taxpayers under the current Labor government paying you financial subsidies to buy into solar and battery is a bad policy.
By the way there is so much energy overloading the grid at those hours. Increases are nothing more than corporate greed. Just look at Globird's new plans. Not to mention unethical behaviour by black listimg customers if they have left for a better deal and later want to return. Thays what happens when you privatise essential infrastructure.
[Graffin] @tman2020: Without getting into a political debate, I feel they were a tad irresponsible with the home battery scheme. Many people bought 40-50kWh batteries (more than they needed) at massively discounted prices, so they have more capacity to store/sell and make a profit with.
I got in early with a Powerwall which only has 13kWh capacity and subsidies weren’t available.
No, I’m not bitter about paying more for my system (it’s more than paid for itself by now), just saying they shouldn’t have offered the rebates on larger batteries (which I believe they’ve now done).
[tman2020] @Graffin: As i said its corporate greed. For example, Globird has reduce FiT, increased usage charges and also increase the daily supply charge. So they are buying for significantly less and seeling at a premium. The three hours free has got more to do with PV dumping energy back into the grid than batteries. Providers are losing nothing during that period esp when most people our at an about between 11am and 2pm.
[JinXR] @tman2020: All the battery owners are filling up during this period (between 30-45kwh) though. Even though its 1-4c/kwh, it still adds to their bottom line.
I smash my heater, do all my dish washing, laundry, plus fill up the battery to 100% in this period.
[dufflover] @Graffin:
I got in early with a Powerwall which only has 13kWh capacity and subsidies weren’t available.
We bought our system when we moved house not that long ago, then less than 6 months after all set up and done they announced the policy! Don't get me wrong I think it's good green energy policy even if the initial boundaries were too loose. It's just very annoying to think at the difference in battery capacity (primarily) that we could've gotten for the same price, and all the quicker ROI and riding it out through winter that comes with it.
[JinXR] Yep mine has gone up 10% - both the kwh charge and the daily lol.
[boomramada] This is not a deal, agl already doing it. Make sure to compare rates before sign up.
[rhino86] There you go, 3 hours to fire up your electric distiller and distill for free.
Get a timer installed for your hot water system as well :)
[Graffin] I'm guessing this won't be available on Amber, and if so, what will it mean for those of us who charge up on cheaper rates between 10am and 3pm… will it negatively affect us by pushing those rates up (even slightly, because there'll be less renewable energy in the grid)?
Okay, Amber isn't offering it, they "don't expect much will change" for Amber customers.
Just in case anyone else on Amber wants a read… https://www.amber.com.au/blog/free-power-at-lunchtime-sounds…
[starionx] The property must not be supplied through an embedded network
Yep. It's bad enough that we can't change plans or provider, but now they're bending us over with this bullshit… fml
[saltysalt] You can thank greedy developers for that one. Don’t see why it’s the governments fault.
[Brisbane1] How do i know if my meter is smart or dumb 🤔
[ruddj] Does it have any antennas connected to it?
[3dprintdeals] Are your daily usage charts estimates or accurate in your power provider app.
[rodericb] Usually if it has a metal disc mounted horizontally which turns, and you have what looks like dials on the front that's a 'dumb' meter. If there is a digital display it will be an interval meter or a 'smart' meter. Some companies will define smart meters as being interval meters which have also have remote reading and energisation/de-energisation. They key is the recording of consumption in intervals through the day.
[shutuptakemymoney101] Time of use meters are the devil's work ;-)
[reece8] how will retailers differentiate and compete for customers if all have to provide 3hrs free window? guess it'll come down to daily supply charge and off and on peak pricing outside the free window?
[Aureus] Retailers only have to offer one "Solar Sharer" plan. Outside of that they can offer whatever they want to differentiate themselves.
[NinjaChicken] I'm not sure there are too many advantages to these rates. There are a number of retailers (Ovo, GloBird, CovaU…) that already have plans which offer more than 24Kwh / Day free for use between 11am - 2pm daily.
I guess there are some areas the above retailers don't service.
But as mentioned you need to compare rates…
[Lets Go Brandon] I'd rather $275 cheaper electricity than gimmicks such as this.
[Choc0] That's like $8 worth of free power every day. You just need to find somewhere to put it. You'd get more than $275 just by heating your hot water.
[Xizor] I'd rather politicians drive policies that dont cause power prices to spiral upwards radically higher than wage growth. But then i guess we'd need a voter base with enough intellect to join those dots together.
[zooby] Time to do some math so I ride the limits of my old house's single phase wiring. A few 2000w heaters cranked in every room to charge the thermal mass, but I better put a sign on the kettle so no one flicks it on in that time window.
[MrGareth] For people from SA - what's the best comparison website for electricity? My mum is paying the "lazy tax" through one of the big retailers (though I'm not sure which) but I'd like to see what decent plans are out there… though it sounds like it's going to be expensive no matter what.
[BargainBenno] I read in another forum someone has a dedicated 7kwh socket to charge their battery. They told me that in 3 hours they will be able to fully charge their 20kw battery. So won't pay for any usage for the day at their home. Just the supply charge. Is this loophole something the government has thought about?
[Choc0] Retailers are jacking up their supply charges across the board. Battery owners have been using a lot less electricity and the retailers need their pound of flesh.
[Xizor] Nothing loop hole about it. They want battery owners to draw power when the grid has excess available during the day, and not draw from the grid during peak times because wind is unreliable/intermittent, solar needs the sun, and they are gradually shutting down the coal power stations so delivering consistent reliable power generation is getting alot more complicated and costly. This is the market reality.
[JinXR] This is partly why electricity prices have gone north.
Mine has gone up 10% - both the kwh charge and the daily.
Churning away, but not good.
[Xizor] They increase due to the cost volatility of renewable power generation being complicated to manage, ongoing capital expenditure to adapt the grid to that, and making a buck for their billionaire shareholders like mike cannon brooks. There is immense profiteering in the never ending rolling out of wind and batteries especially since they dont have anywhere near the life of traditional gas or coal power station so always more needed. Im all for lowering our emissions but reality is its become the biggest pork barrelling piss take at our expense.
[amsaini15] Does anyone know who is the cheapest provider for low daily supply rates after 1st of July 2026? I have solar and batteries so I don't care about the charges.
[morebunnings] Won't know until the 1st July.
I've already messaged a few power companies asking, they won't say what their rates are going to.
My daily supply charge is going up a rediculous amount, so will be switching on the 1st.
[SetTheFaqUp] "Because this is an optional offer, it may not be the best choice for every household, so it is worth comparing the pricing outside the free period with your current electricity plan before switching."
Belongs to forum, not a deal.
Many offer "free usage" plans and both the supply as well usage rates are highway robbery.
[aligab] Well cant use that 3 hrs window since most working families will be at work during those hours
[seniordoc] I don't have a solar panel. Does this apply to me?
Should I use a flat rate or time of use plan?
I am currently with AGL senior saver flat rate plan.
Thank you for advice.
[interstellar] https://www.energy.gov.au/rebates/solar-sharer-offer
You don’t need to have a solar panel. But you do need a smart meter on your site.
[Lucian] Unless you can shift your usage to the three hour period, this won't be better for most people. Its a standing offer so the rates won't be competitive. There are even rules from the regulator that require retailers to warn customers attempting to sign up to this that they might not be better off.
